query: Draft a suit for recovery of Rs.20,00,000/- paid as advance for a business transaction which failed due to Defendant's default. Plead total failure of consideration under Section 65 of Indian Contract Act. Claim refund with interest and costs. Include cause of action paragraph with accrual date, continuing nature of cause of action, valuation, court fee, and proper verification clause. Draft suitable for filing before the District Court.

v4.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE DISTRICT JUDGE (CIVIL) AT {{COURT_PLACE}}

O.S. No. __________ / 20____ - Money Recovery under Section 65, Indian Contract Act, 1872

PLAINTIFF: {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, aged about {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, Residing at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}. 

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

VERSUS

DEFENDANT: {{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, aged about {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years, Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}}, Residing at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}. 

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the Defendant resides at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}} within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Court and the cause of action arose within such territorial limits at the place of the transaction described herein as {{TRANSACTION_PLACE}}.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court. This Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain suits of this nature.

4. This Court has jurisdiction to try and adjudicate the present suit in terms of the relief sought and applicable statutory provisions.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. The Plaintiff is a {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}} residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}} and engaged in {{PLAINTIFF_BUSINESS_DESCRIPTION}}.

6. The Defendant is a {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}} residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}} and known to the Plaintiff through {{RELATIONSHIP_CONTEXT}}.

7. On or about {{ACCRUAL_DATE}}, the Defendant approached the Plaintiff and represented that {{DEFENDANT_REPRESENTATION}}. Based on the said representations, the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into {{TRANSACTION_DESCRIPTION}} on {{TRANSACTION_DATE}} (a true copy annexed as Annexure A).

8. In pursuance of the said {{TRANSACTION_DESCRIPTION}} and in good faith reliance upon the Defendant's representations, the Plaintiff paid Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} on {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} through {{PAYMENT_MODE}} (proof of payment annexed as Annexure B).

9. The Defendant undertook to {{DEFENDANT_OBLIGATION}} on or before {{OBLIGATION_DUE_DATE}}. The Plaintiff duly performed his part of the obligation and fulfilled all conditions precedent to the Defendant's obligation.

10. However, despite the Plaintiff having duly performed his part, the Defendant wholly failed, neglected and refused to {{BREACH_DESCRIPTION}} on {{DEFAULT_DATE}}. The Defendant's failure constitutes a breach of the {{TRANSACTION_DESCRIPTION}} and has caused loss to the Plaintiff.

11. Subsequently, the Plaintiff made repeated oral and written requests to the Defendant on {{FOLLOWUP_DATES}}, urging the Defendant to honor the obligation. Copies of correspondence annexed as Annexure C.

12. Left with no alternative, the Plaintiff, through his advocate, caused a legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} to be served upon the Defendant through registered post A.D., calling upon the Defendant to {{NOTICE_DEMAND}} within 15 days of receipt of the notice. A true copy of the legal notice along with postal receipts is annexed as Annexure D.

13. Despite receipt and acknowledgment of the said legal notice, the Defendant has failed to comply with the demands therein. The Defendant's reply dated {{REPLY_DATE}}, if any, was evasive and did not address the substantive claims (Annexure E). Alternatively, no reply was received within the stipulated period.

15. There is total failure of consideration for the contract in terms of Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, as the advance of Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} was paid for a business transaction which did not fructify due to the Defendant's default and the Defendant did not render any value in return. The Defendant has retained the amount without lawful justification, causing loss to the Plaintiff.

LEGAL BASIS

15. The Plaintiff submits that the Defendant is liable under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, on account of total failure of consideration. The facts show that the Plaintiff advanced Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} for a business transaction which did not fructify due to the Defendant's default, and the Defendant did not render any value.

16. Without prejudice to the above, the Plaintiff pleads in the alternative under Section 70 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 that the Defendant unjustly retained the money and is bound to restore it; In the alternative, Section 72 may be invoked for damages for non-performance.

17. It is further contended that the suit is within the period of limitation under Article 24 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, as accrual occurred when the money was received and the suit is filed within three years of that date.

CAUSE OF ACTION

18. ORIGIN: The cause of action first arose on {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} when the Defendant received Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} as an advance for the transaction and the consideration subsequently failed due to the Defendant's default.

19. FURTHER ACCRUAL: The cause of action further arose on {{DEFAULT_DATE}} when the Defendant refused to refund or perform, despite the Plaintiff's requests and the service of the legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}}.

20. CONTINUING NATURE: The cause of action is continuing as the Defendant retains the Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} without restoration to the Plaintiff, causing ongoing loss.

LIMITATION

21. Article 24 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963 governs the period of three years for suits for money payable by the defendant to the plaintiff for money received by the defendant for the plaintiff's use. The accrual date is {{ACCRUAL_DATE}}, and the suit has been instituted within the prescribed period.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

22. The suit is valued for pecuniary jurisdiction and court fees at Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh Only). The Court Fee payable is calculated as per the applicable Court Fees Act and the Schedule thereto; the precise Court Fee amount is to be determined under the relevant ad valorem slab and paid in accordance with law. Court Fee context: {{COURT_FEE_CONTEXT}}.

INTEREST

23. INTEREST:

    (a) Pre-suit interest: at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum on Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} from {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} till the date of filing this suit, amounting to approximately Rs. {{PRE_SUIT_INTEREST_AMOUNT}}.

    (b) Pendente lite interest: at {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum or such rate as this Court deems just and reasonable under Section 34 CPC during the pendency of the suit.

    (c) Future interest: at {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from the date of decree till realisation.

PRAYER

24. In the aforesaid premises, the Plaintiff respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to:

    (a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for the principal amount of Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}} (Rupees Twenty Lakh Only);
(b) Award pre-suit interest on the said amount from {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} till the date of filing this suit;
(c) Award pendente lite interest under Section 34 CPC during the pendency of the suit;
(d) Award future interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from the date of decree till realisation;
(e) Award costs of this suit including court fees and pre-litigation costs incurred for issuing the legal notice; and
    (f) Grant such other and further relief as this Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

AND FOR THIS ACT OF KINDNESS, THE PLAINTIFF SHALL, AS IN DUTY BOUND, EVER PRAY.

VERIFICATION

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby solemnly verify and state on oath that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 25 of this plaint are true and correct to my personal knowledge based upon the records and documents available with me, that the contents relating to legal submissions are based upon legal advice received and are believed to be correct, that no part of the cause of action has arisen within the jurisdiction of any other court, and that nothing material has been concealed therefrom. Verified at {{COURT_PLACE}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

                                                        {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

                                                        PLAINTIFF

Through:
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate
Enrollment No. {{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT}}
{{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}

v5.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE DISTRICT JUDGE (COMMERCIAL DIVISION)
AT {{COURT_PLACE}}

Commercial Suit No. __________ / 20____

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}},
Aged about {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

{{DEFENDANT_NAME}},
Aged about {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF Rs. 20,00,000/- (RUPEES TWENTY LAKHS ONLY) WITH INTEREST AND COSTS UNDER SECTION 65 OF THE INDIAN CONTRACT ACT, 1872

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court (Commercial Division) has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Court in relation to the advance paid for the business transaction at {{TRANSACTION_PLACE}} and the Defendant resides within the jurisdiction of this Court.

3. This Hon'ble Court (Commercial Division) has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Court. This Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain suits of this nature.

FACTS OF THE CASE

4. The Plaintiff is a {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}} residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}} and undertook a business transaction with the Defendant for which the Plaintiff paid Rs. 20,00,000/- as an advance. The details of the transaction are set out in Annexure A.

5. The Defendant received the aforesaid amount as advance for the contemplated transaction and undertook to perform certain obligations as part of the arrangement; the terms and conditions, including the purpose and scope of the advance, are described in Annexure A.

6. The Plaintiff incurred an expenditure and performed his part by advancing Rs. 20,00,000/- on {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} through {{PAYMENT_MODE}}. Proof of such payment is annexed herewith as Annexure B.

7. The Defendant's obligation under the arrangement was to {{DEFENDANT_OBLIGATION}} by {{OBLIGATION_DUE_DATE}}. The Plaintiff fulfilled all conditions precedent to the Defendant's obligation and performed his part in full.

8. Despite the Plaintiff's full performance, the Defendant defaulted and refused to {{BREACH_DESCRIPTION}} or to refund the amount, leading to total failure of consideration under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872. The Defendant's default has deprived the Plaintiff of the value of the advance paid.

9. The Plaintiff made repeated requests for refund and issued reminders, copies of which are annexed as Annexure C. Despite these reminders, the Defendant has not refunded the amount nor offered any lawful justification for retention.

10. In pursuance of the above, the Plaintiff, through his advocate, caused a legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} to be served upon the Defendant through registered post A.D., calling upon the Defendant to refund Rs. 20,00,000/- within 15 days of receipt of the notice (a true copy of the legal notice with postal receipt and acknowledgment card is annexed hereto and marked as Annexure D).

11. The Defendant's non-compliance with the demands in the said legal notice continues, and no part of the advance has been refunded. The Defendant’s reply, if any, is annexed as Annexure E, or in the alternative there has been no reply within the stipulated period, evidencing a clear intention not to honour the obligation.

12. As a direct and proximate consequence of the Defendant's breach, the Plaintiff has suffered (i) the principal amount of Rs. 20,00,000/-; (ii) loss of opportunity to employ the said amount in business at prevailing commercial rates; and (iii) mental agony, harassment and inconvenience. The Plaintiff seeks relief by way of refund of the advance amount with interest and costs.

LEGAL BASIS

13. The Plaintiff submits that the Defendant is liable under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872, inasmuch as the Plaintiff paid Rs. 20,00,000/- as advance for a business transaction and the entire consideration has failed due to the Defendant's default. Section 65 provides that where money is paid for a consideration which afterwards fails, the person who received the money is bound to restore it. In the present case, the Defendant received the amount and failed to render any consideration, thereby entitling the Plaintiff to recover the amount along with costs and interest.

14. In the alternative, without prejudice to the above, the Plaintiff submits that the Defendant would be liable under Sections 70 and 72 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 for total failure of consideration and related consequences, but the Plaintiff limits his relief to Section 65 as the primary basis for recovery.

CAUSE OF ACTION

15. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on the date of receipt of Rs. 20,00,000/- by the Defendant, i.e., {{ACCRUAL_DATE}}, in pursuance of the business transaction, and the Defendant undertook to perform, but failed to do so, resulting in total failure of consideration.

16. The cause of action is a continuing one since the Defendant continues to retain the entire advance amount without any lawful justification and has not refunded the same. The Plaintiff is therefore entitled to sue for recovery of the amount and costs, with interest, on a continuing basis.

LIMITATION

17. The suit is within the period of three years prescribed under Article 24 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963. The accrual occurred on the date when the Defendant received the consideration, i.e., on {{ACCRUAL_DATE}}; the suit has been filed within the prescribed period.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

18. The suit is valued at Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. The requisite court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- has been paid on this plaint, computed on the suit valuation at the applicable ad valorem rate.

INTEREST

19. The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount from the accrual date {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} till the date of decree/realisation at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems just and reasonable, under the provisions of the Indian Contract Act and the CPC, as applicable.

PRAYER

20. In the premises stated above, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to:

    (a) Decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for the refund of Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakhs Only), being the principal amount paid as advance;

    (b) Award interest on the said sum at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from {{ACCRUAL_DATE}} till the date of decree and realisation, as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and just;

    (c) Award costs of this suit including court fees, advocates' fees, and all pre-litigation costs incurred for issuing the legal notice and other related steps; and

    (d) Grant such other and further relief as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

AND FOR THIS ACT OF KINDNESS, THE PLAINTIFF SHALL, AS IN DUTY BOUND, EVER PRAY.

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A – Copy of the transaction document/arrangement.
Annexure B – Proof of payment (payment receipt/bank statement).
Annexure C – Reminders/communications.
Annexure D – Legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} with postal receipt and acknowledgment card.
Annexure E – Section 12A pre-institution mediation certificate (if applicable).

VERIFICATION

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby solemnly verify and state on oath that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 20 of this plaint are true and correct to the best of my knowledge and belief, that the facts stated herein are true and based on records and documents available with me, that no part of the cause of action has arisen in the jurisdiction of any other court, and that nothing material has been concealed therefrom. Verified at {{COURT_PLACE}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

                                                        {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

                                                        PLAINTIFF

Through:
{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate
Enrollment No. {{ADVOCATE_ENROLLMENT}}
{{ADVOCATE_ADDRESS}}
